I'll go first — my change is purely additive: new __init__ params (debug: bool=False, debug_logger=None), a self._logger init, and guarded 'if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)' log calls inserted at existing trace points (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset). I will NOT rename/move/reorder any existing methods, change method signatures besides __init__, or touch idle_timeout/tick logic. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact insertion points once finished (should be quick). Please hold off editing streaming_listener.py until you see my DONE, then add your idle_timeout_s/tick() on top of my version. If that works for you, ACCEPT; otherwise propose an alternative ordering.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: ACCEPT. Co-owning with disjoint regions: I only touch __init__ (add debug/debug_logger params + self._logger init), and insert guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at existing trace points (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset) without altering control flow. To avoid colliding on the same insertion point in __init__, let's fix an order: in the signature, place new params as ...existing..., debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None, idle_timeout_s=... (yours last); in the body, place new attrs as ...existing assignments..., self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger(...), then your self.idle_timeout_s / self._last_chunk_ts lines last. I will not touch receive()'s control flow or add a tick() method. Implementing now, will send DONE shortly.
